publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-17 00:02:55 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection consists of notes and early material concerning medieval French literature, Italian literature, Le Philosophe and the early 18th century, Diderot, Voltaire and Rousseau.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of French and comparative literature, Cornell University.

Herbert Dieckmann was born in Germany, and received his Ph.D. from the University of Bonn. He was a member of the Harvard University faculty, and served as chairman of Harvard's Department of Romance Languages. Dieckmann was noted for his research on French philosopher and essayist Denis Diderot. In 1948, he discovered the Diderot manuscripts and made the first critical inventory of the collection published in 1951 as the "Inventaire du Fonds Vandeul et Inedits de Diderot."

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of notes and early material concerning medieval French literature, Italian literature, Le Philosophe and the early 18th century, Diderot, Voltaire and Rousseau; notes and papers on French philosophy, art, and literature; course and lecture notes; also, research notes, drafts, and files on Diderot, including a new Diderot edition with pertinent correspondence and volume 9 - Interpretation of Nature; books; reprints; and a small amount of correspondence, 1956-1975.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Herbert Dieckmann Papers, #14-31-2150. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Epic Poetry, lyric poetry - Marie de France
Old French - Roman Courtois, Chretien de Troyes, Concept of Love
Old French - Linguistics - Vie de St. Alexis
Kolleg Mittelalter - also Epentheorie, epic poetry, and other notes
Old French - Sainte Eulalia, Vie de St. Alexis
Material for Le Philosophe and the Early 18th Century
General theory of the philosophe, conception of philosophy
Vartanian - review of book - on l'homme machine
André Morize "Paul Valéry - Discours sur Voltaire - Sorbonne, Dec. 10, 1944"
Sur une êdition mal conne … ouerres complètes de Voltaire, May 1913 (?)
Texte. J. J. Rousseau et le Cosmopoliton
IV Le Sentiment de la nature Rousseau botaniste
Series II. Alphabetical File of Authors
Bayle - Paper on Elements of Style
Bayle - General Program for conne (?) papers and identified notes for the exam. - philo systems
La Bruyère - note card file and notebook
La Rochefoucauld - alphabetical note card file a-m
Series III. Aesthetics - 17th and 18th Century French Literature
Theory of Lyric (materials not in article)
Material - various stages of [History of an Idea]
Colloque d'Aix - La villeau XVIII siecle
Cassurer - The Philosophy of the Enlightenment
Le philosophique à la mode et les Lois de Minos
Material on Doctrine Classique and Beauty
Art and Thought in the 18th Century (Rococo Lecture) English Version (as given in Columbus) Article for Wellek Festschrift Lecture for MLA
Little Autograph Sheets (some words still to be diciphered)
Autograph Letters - Diderot, Grimm, Mme. d'Epinay
Diderot a.l.s. to M. le Breton
Diderot's conception of language - additional material
Drama criticism - Entretiens sur le Fils Naturel de la poesie dramatique
Lecture - Form and Expression in Diderot's Work
Lettre apologétique de l'abbé Raynal à M. Grimm
Lettre apologétique de l'abbé Raynal à M. Grimm
Lettre sur les sourds et muets
Lettre sur les sourds et muets
Ms. Sources for Elements de physiologie - photos of slips of paper - Diderot's hand
Observations sur la statue de Marc-Aurèle
Réflexions sur le livre de l'Esprit de Helvétius
Textual criticism (notes for lecture in D. Mss.)
Reviews of books by or concerning Diderot
Series V. Diderot Edition Correspondence and Notes
Series VI. Diderot Vol. 9 - Interpretation of Nature Manuscript
Review of Helmut Hatzfeld's Rococo for Yearbook of Comparative and General Literature
Reprint from the Art Bulletin, Vol. XLVII, No. 3
Series VIII. Course and Lecture Notes
B - In Praise of Knowledge - Bacon
Enlightenment - Manuel, Vyverberg, Phillip, Spink, etc.
Examen de la Religion - le Philosopher and related problems
Existentialism - material not used in article in Yale French Studies
The Relationship between Philosophy and Literature in 18th Century France
French 121 - Historism - Modern humanism and tradition
